title: NPTEL IoT 2025 JAN

questions.question: IoT stands for ____________.
questions.detailed_solution: The full form of IoT is "Internet of Things"
questions.reference: See lecture 1 (Introduction to IoT - Part - I) @ 1:30

questions.question: Which of the following technologies have unified and has resulted in the evolution of IoT?
questions.detailed_solution:
Unification of technologies which has resulted in the advancement of
IoT are -
a. Low-power embedded systems
b. Cloud Computing
c. Big Data
d. Machine Learning
e. Networking
questions.reference: See lecture 1 (Introduction to IoT - Part - I) @ 5:54

questions.question: Which of the following are the enables of IoT?
questions.detailed_solution:
The enables of IoT are -
a. RFID
b. Nanotechnology
c. Sensors
d. Smart Networks

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of an IoT LAN?
questions.detailed_solution:
The functionalities of an IoT Gateway are -
a. Local, short-range communication
b. Spreads across buildings or organization

questions.question: State whether the following statement is True or False. Statement: The integration of existing devices, smart devices, and constrained nodes in a singular framework is one of the reasons for the address crunch in IoT.
questions.detailed_solution:
The integration of existing devices, smart devices, and constrained
nodes in a singular framework is one of the reasons for the address crunch in IoT.

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "In Multi-homing, a node/network is connected to a single network for improved reliability.
questions.detailed_solution:
In multi-homing, a node/network is connected to multiple networks for
improved reliability.

questions.question: Which of the following is/are the approach/approaches for multi-homing?
questions.detailed_solution:
The following are the approaches for multi-homing -
1. Proxy-based approach
2. Gateway-based approach

questions.question: IPv6 uses ____________ notation for its representation.
questions.detailed_solution: IPv6 uses Hexadecimal notation for its representation.

questions.question: State True or False. The parameters sensed by a sensor may be sent to the cloud for further processing.
questions.detailed_solution:
The parameters sensed by a sensor may be sent to the cloud for further
processing.

questions.question: The IPv6 notation uses ___________________ number of bits to represent an address.
questions.detailed_solution: The IPv6 notation uses 128 bits to represent an address.

questions.question: A sensor is -

options.text: Only sensitive to the measured property

questions.detailed_solution:

b. Data type

questions.question: Which of the following is correct statement

options.text: Controlling AC loads using low DC signals

questions.detailed_solution: All the statements given are correct.

questions.detailed_solution: Based on the output, sensors are classified as Digital and Analog.

questions.question: Soft actuators are -
questions.detailed_solution: Soft actuators are polymer based.

questions.question: Based on functionality, MQTT is a _______ protocol.
questions.detailed_solution: MQTT is a Data Protocol.

questions.question: MQTT is designed for -
questions.detailed_solution:
MQTT is designed for -
1. Remote connections

questions.question: State True or False. MQTT protocol follows _____________ paradigm for exchanging messages.
questions.detailed_solution: MQTT protocol follows the publish-subscribe paradigm for exchanging messages.

questions.detailed_solution: In MQTT, the Publishers are lightweight sensors.

questions.question: Which of the following is MQTT component?
questions.detailed_solution:

Publisher - Cambridge University Press, Edition - 1 (2021)

questions.question: State True or False. There are only two methods specified by the MQTT protocol.
questions.detailed_solution: There are 5 number of methods in MQTT protocol.

questions.detailed_solution:

communication system into seven layers.

questions.question: The "Destination Address" in the IPv4 packet represents which of the following?

options.text: The source node address of the packet
The intermediate hop in the network

questions.detailed_solution:
The "Destination Address" in the IPv4 packet represents the address of
the destination node in the network.

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "WirelessHART is the latest release of Highway Addressable Remote Transducer protocol."
questions.detailed_solution:
WirelessHART is the latest release of Highway Addressable Remote
Transducer protocol.

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "Wired HART has a network layer."
questions.detailed_solution: Wired HART does not have a network layer.

questions.question: State true or false: "WirelessHART physical layer is derived from 802.15.2 protocol"
questions.detailed_solution: WirelessHART physical layer is derived from 802.15.4 protocol.

questions.question: WirelessHART operates only in __________ GHz ISM band.
questions.detailed_solution: HART operates only in the 2.4 GHz ISM band.

questions.question: HART standard was developed from _____________ smart field devices.
questions.detailed_solution: HART standard was developed for networked smart field devices.

questions.question: Main difference between wired and unwired versions is in the physical, data link, and ______ layers.
questions.detailed_solution:
Main difference between wired and unwired versions is in the physical,
data link, and network layers.

questions.question: State true or false "Collision free and deterministic communication is achieved in HART's data link layer."
questions.detailed_solution:
Collision free and deterministic communication is achieved in
HART's data link layer.

questions.question: Channel hopping is incorporated in which layer of HART?
questions.detailed_solution: Channel hopping is incorporated in the data link layer of HART.

questions.question: State True or False. "The HART application layer is responsible for extracting commands from a message, executing it and generating responses."
questions.detailed_solution:
The HART application layer is responsible for extracting commands
from a message, executing it and generating responses.

questions.question: NFC is designed for use by devices within ________________ to each other.
questions.detailed_solution:
NFC is designed for use by devices within close proximity to each
other.

questions.question: Passive NFC devices ___________ information which is _____________ by other devices.
questions.detailed_solution:
Passive NFC devices contain information which is read by other
devices.

questions.question: State True or False. "NFC devices work on the principle of magnetic induction."
questions.detailed_solution: NFC devices work on the principle of magnetic induction.

questions.question: Bluetooth technology is based on _________________.
questions.detailed_solution: Bluetooth technology is based on Ad-hoc piconets.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: The Link Manager Protocol in Bluetooth manages the only establishment and authentication.
questions.detailed_solution:
The Link Manager Protocol in Bluetooth manages the establishment,
authentication, link configuration.

questions.question: Zigbee commonly uses __________data rate.
questions.detailed_solution: Zigbee commonly uses a 250-kbps data rate.

questions.question: Humans carry their devices and move around. Sensors embedded within the devices record readings. Sensory readings are then transmitted for processing. This paradigm of sensing is known as -
questions.detailed_solution:

questions.detailed_solution:
Use of different programming languages such as JavaScript, Python, JAVA, and others

questions.detailed_solution:
The interoperability between devices and device users in terms of

questions.question: What is the full form of UMB in IoT interoperability?

questions.question: What does the following code do?

options.text: Blink 3 times with 1000ms ON and 500ms OFF
Blink 3 times with 500ms ON and 500ms OFF
Blink 3 times with 1000ms ON and 1000ms OFF

text: Blink 3 times with 1000ms ON and 500ms OFF

questions.detailed_solution:
The LED is turned ON for 1000ms using delay(1000).
The LED is turned OFF for 500ms using delay(500).
This process repeats 3 times in the for loop.
Thus, the LED blinks 3 times with 1000ms ON and 500ms OFF.
questions.answer_display: Blink 3 times with 1000ms ON and 500ms OFF

questions.question: How many types of loops will you find in Arduino Programming?

Like C programming Arduino sketches also have 3 types of loops,
for, while and do-while loops.

questions.question: Consider the following piece of Python code. What is the output?
questions.code_block:
x = [4, 5, 6]
y = [str(x[0] + 1), str(len(x) * 2) + '&Code']
z = y[1].split('&')
print(z[1])
questions.detailed_solution:
1. x = [4, 5, 6]: A list with three elements.
2. y = [str(x[0] + 1), str(len(x) * 2) + '&Code']:
o x[0] is 4, so x[0] + 1 is 5, and str(x[0] + 1) becomes "5".
o len(x) is 3, so len(x) * 2 is 6, and str(len(x) * 2) + '&Code' becomes "6&Code".
o Therefore, y = ["5", "6&Code"].
3. z = y[1].split('&'):
o y[1] is "6&Code".
o Splitting "6&Code" by '&' gives ['6', 'Code'].
4. z[1] is 'Code'.
Thus, the print(z[1]) statement outputs Code.

questions.question: State True or False. Statement: "To indicate different blocks of code, Python follows rigid indentation."
questions.detailed_solution: To indicate different blocks of code, Python follows rigid indentation.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I @ 7::29).

Reading from the file
Writing data

questions.answer_display: To provide a platform for executing virtual machines

questions.question: Which of the following is a limitation of SaaS?
Fill in the blank. ____________ means independent of device or location.
questions.detailed_solution: Ubiquitous means independent of device or location.

questions.question: Which of the following is/are the advantages of cloud computing?
questions.detailed_solution:
The advantages of cloud computing include Elasticity, Pay-per-use and Self-
Service.

questions.question: Fill in the blanks. Fog computing is an intermediate layer between __________ and _________.
questions.detailed_solution:
Fog computing is an intermediate layer between Cloud
and devices.

questions.question: The managerial role is played by __________ in sensor-cloud architecture.
questions.detailed_solution: Sensor-Cloud Service Provider plays the managerial role in sensor-cloud architecture.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a component of OpenStack?
questions.detailed_solution: Nova and Swift are two of the many components of OpenStack

questions.question: Who coined the term Fog computing?
questions.detailed_solution: CISCO coined the term Fog computing.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. The concept of ________ enables physical hardware to be shared among multiple entities.
questions.detailed_solution:
The concept of hardware virtualization enables physical hardware to be shared
among multiple entities. (Please refer to Page 262, Chapter 11, Introduction to IoT. S. Misra, A.
Mukherjee, and A. Roy, 2020. Cambridge University Press.)

questions.question: Openstack is a free open source software for cloud framework simulation and experimentation with various cloud applications.
questions.detailed_solution:
Openstack is a free open source software for cloud framework simulation
and experimentation with various cloud applications. It can be downloaded and installed for
free.

questions.question: In IoT, temporal sensitivity of data DOES NOT play an important role
questions.detailed_solution:
IoT data can be classified in to time sensitive data, less time sensitive data and data not
sensitive to time. Hence time sensitivity plays a big role in IOT data classification.

questions.question: Which among the following is NOT a component of OpenStack.
questions.detailed_solution:
Stellar is not a component of OpenStack. The rest are various components, including
Nova, Glance, Swift etc.

questions.question: Which among the following is the principal feature of sensor clouds, with respect to sensor nodes?
questions.detailed_solution:
Sesnor virtualization is the principal feature of sensor clouds and their
utility.
questions.reference: Refer lecture 42 and 43 on Sensor cloud

questions.question: The optimal composition of __________ is a management issue in sensor-cloud.
questions.detailed_solution:
The optimal composition of Virtual sensor nodes is a management issue in
sensor-cloud.

questions.question: How many different types of caching mechanism are there in sensor cloud?
questions.detailed_solution:
Internal Cache (IC) and External Cache (EC) are two different types of caching
mechanisms used in sensor cloud.

questions.question: Data from an IoT device is transferred to cloud via a network, which is then processed at the cloud and then a response is sent back to the IoT device from the cloud after processing. The time it takes for one- way data transfer between the node and cloud is 10s and the data processing time at the cloud is 'x' seconds. It takes a total of 25s for the entire to and fro transfer of data between the sensor and cloud along with processing at the cloud. What is the value of x?
questions.detailed_solution:
Time taken for one-way data transfer between the node and cloud is 10s. Total time
taken for the data transfer is 25s. So 25=10+x+10 (transfer from node to cloud+processing at
cloud+transfer from cloud to node). Thus x=5s.

questions.question: Which among the following is true?

options.text: Fog computing acts as a complement to cloud computing
Fog computing is a replacement for cloud computing
Fog computing and cloud computing are the same
Fog computing is more powerful than cloud computing (with respect to resources)

text: Fog computing acts as a complement to cloud computing

questions.detailed_solution:
Fog computing and cloud computing are complementary technologies.
Fog helps in bringing the cloud closer to the IoT devices.
questions.answer_display: Fog computing acts as a complement to cloud computing

questions.question: Which component of OpenStack do you use to access all the other components?
questions.detailed_solution:
Horizon is the dashboard of OpenStack which provides the GUI and from
where you
can access other components.

questions.question: Which among the following are active connected entities in a holistic smart city environment?
With the help of ICT tools, it is possible to increase and improve citizen participation for a good governance based smart city
Which among the following is a possible challenge with respect to smart parking lots?
questions.detailed_solution: All of the given options are indeed potential challenges as well.

questions.question: What refers to combining information from multiple sensor sources?
questions.detailed_solution: Multi-sensor data fusion combines information from multiple sensor sources.

questions.question: Which of the following is one of the theory of evidence-based mathematical methods of data fusion?
questions.detailed_solution:
Belief function is one of the theory of evidence-based mathematical methods of data
fusion.

questions.question: With respect to data fusion from multiple IoT sensors, does outlier data present with a challenge?

questions.detailed_solution:
The phases of ICV development: Phase 1: Based on 2G, Phase 2: Based on 4G
LTE, Phase 3: Vehicles connected to cloud

questions.question: With which of the following can the decision-making gap between the sensors and the actuators be bridged.

Which of the following statements are true about the HAN standards? Statement I: Physical and MAC layers are defined by IEEE802.15.4. Statement II: Network layer is defined by Zigbee. Statement III: Application layer is defined by IEEE802.15.4
questions.detailed_solution:
Physical and MAC layers are defined by IEEE802.15.4. Network layer
and Application layers are defined by Zigbee.

questions.question: What is UPnP?
What are the disadvantages of V2X communication?
questions.detailed_solution:
Disadvantages of V2X communication includes tracking of movement,
violation of privacy, loss of data control, etc.

questions.question: Mobility of vehicles in a V2X environment limit which of the following that restricts the use of TCP/IP for V2X communication?

options.text: Backbone routing in IP core networks

questions.detailed_solution:
TCP/IP works best with localized data, which is not present in V2X environments. This
restricts the use of TCP/IP for V2X communication.

questions.question: In VANET, link durations are long and easily scaled-up to include all the vehicles on the road?
questions.detailed_solution: Link durations are short due to the highly dynamic nature of VANETs.

questions.question: CCN is derived from ICN architecture.
questions.detailed_solution:
CCN (Content Centric Networking) is derived from Information Centric Networking
(ICN)

questions.question: In an ICV environment, efficient cooperation between different vehicles on a highway can lead to a safer scenario with respect to collision and avoiding incoming traffic.
Which among the following is the most probable application scenario for a smart grid?
With respect to a smart grid, which stakeholders are potentially benefitted?

options.text: Both energy service provides and consumers
Neither energy service providers nor consumers

text: Both energy service provides and consumers

questions.answer_display: Both energy service provides and consumers

questions.question: It is not possible to perform load forecasting using a smart grid.
questions.detailed_solution: Load forecasting is possible in a smart grid.

questions.question: With respect to the following Smart Grid architecture, which among the following is the most probable utility of the IP network?

options.text: IP network is used to connect smart grid components globally for seamless data transfer
IP network is used for multimedia video streaming only
IP network is used to induce more congestion

text: IP network is used to connect smart grid components globally for seamless data transfer

questions.answer_display: IP network is used to connect smart grid components globally for seamless data transfer

questions.question: Smart Home is an isolated concept and is not integrable with smart grid?
Which of the following is a cloud application of smart grid?
questions.detailed_solution:
Energy management, information management and security are all the cloud
applications in smart grid

questions.question: Which of the following is not a vulnerability of Smart Grid?
questions.detailed_solution:
Integrity, physical threats and dynamic system attacks are all
the vulnerabilities of smart grids

questions.question: IIoT corresponds to which industrial revolution?
IIoT is inherently data intensive.
questions.detailed_solution:
In IIoT, big data analytics plays an important part, hence it is inherently data
intensive.

questions.question: Which among the following is one of the requirements and utility of IIoT?
Smart Grid follows which type of flow of energy?
questions.detailed_solution: Smart grid follows bidirectional flow of energy.

questions.question: What is the Flow of data?
questions.detailed_solution:
The flow of the data is Generation, Acquisition, Storage and
Analysis

questions.question: Intelligent transport system is least likely to render which of the following connectivity?
questions.detailed_solution:
ITS provides Vehicle-to-vehicle connectivity, Vehicle-to-sensor connectivity, Vehicle-
to-road infrastructure and Vehicle-to-internet connectivity.

questions.question: Approximately around ________ of the total data available currently in the world is unstructured.
questions.detailed_solution: Unstructured data accounts for 80% of the total data available today in the world.

Processing the sensed data on the device itself is known as in-place
approach.

questions.detailed_solution:
Processing the handheld activity device data with artificial intelligence can be
used for detecting sudden fall of a person.

questions.question: Which of the following is an assumption of ANOVA?
questions.detailed_solution:
ANOVA assumes the following:
Homogeneity of variances: The variances within each group should be approximately equal.
Normality: The response variable should follow a normal distribution within groups.
Independence: Observations should be independent of each other. These assumptions are critical
for the validity of ANOVA results. (Refer to

questions.question: Select the statement(s) that denote the type of ANOVA. Statement I: One way analysis Statement II: Two way analysis Statement III: K-way analysis
questions.detailed_solution: The types of ANOVA includes One way analysis, Two way analysis and K-way analysis

questions.detailed_solution:
The AmbuSense is a strictly privacy-aware system with patient-identity
masking.
